sections.paragraph:
διαίρεσις, εως, ἡ(s. διαιρέω) gener. a state or circumstance of being divided up

① division or distribution of someth. to pers., apportionment, division (Hdt. et al.; ins, pap; Jdth 9:4; Sir 14:15; Philo) διαιρέσεις χαρισμάτων allotments of spiritual gifts 1 Cor 12:4; cp. vss. 5, 6 (this interpr. is supported by vs. 11 διαιροῦν ἑκάστῳ καθὼς βούλεται). Another tenable interpr. for this passage is

② a state of difference in the nature of objects or events, difference, variety (Pla., Soph. 267b τίνα μείζω διαίρεσιν ἀγνωσίας τε καὶ γνώσεως θήσομεν; Lucian, Hermot. 52; Epict. 2, 6, 24; Tat. 12, 1; Ath. 10, 3; 12, 2, R. 71, 16).

③ division through violent action, tearing apart (of political conflicts TestJud 22:1 διαιρέσεις κατʼ ἀλλήλων) of the body in certain kinds of torture and execution IRo 5:3 (text uncertain; s. ἀνατομή; cp. Artem. 1, 67 p. 62, 7 διαιρέσεις ὑπὸ σιδήρου; Philo, Agr. 129 ἄνευ τομῆς κ. διαιρέσεως).—DELG s.v. αἱρέω. M-M. TW. Sv.

gener. gener. = generally

someth. someth. = something

pers. pers. = person(s)

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

interpr. interpr. = interpreted, interpretation

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Agr Agr = Agraphon (non-canonical sayings of Jesus)—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 229
